gaps.description: Vision mandates agentic-lite = agentic-core (LLM loop) + agentic-filesystem + agentic-shell. No agentic-core dependency exists — agent loop, provider abstraction, tool schema, and streaming are all self-contained in src/ask.ts (~140 lines)
Design principle: ask.ts should be < 100 lines (薄层). Current ask.ts is 140 lines with full agent loop logic that should be delegated to agentic-core
Streaming support: vision lists streaming as part of agentic-core's responsibility. No streaming implementation exists in the codebase
Provider layer should be delegated to agentic-core per vision. Currently src/providers/ implements Anthropic, OpenAI, and custom adapters directly in agentic-lite
toolConfig.code.timeout declared in types.ts:30 but never enforced in code.ts executeCode — misbehaving code could hang indefinitely
Browser-side AI agent SDK — single ask() function with tool-use loop fully implemented
file_read / file_write via agentic-filesystem — zero-config MemoryStorage default, browser virtual filesystem support
code_exec in browser sandbox — QuickJS async sandbox with fs injection for JavaScript, auto-detects language, Python via Pyodide (browser) and python3 (Node)
True multi-round agent loop — MAX_TOOL_ROUNDS=10 loop, agent can continuously call tools until task completion
shell_exec (Node-only) with browser guard — adds Node capability without breaking browser users, uses agentic-shell
web_search tool supports Tavily and Serper providers with image results
Browser compatibility — no Node.js static dependencies, all platform-specific code uses dynamic import with guards
Public API exports complete: ask, AgenticConfig, AgenticResult, Provider, ToolName, Source, CodeResult, FileResult, ShellResult, ToolCall from src/index.ts
No heavy frameworks — no chain/graph/memory abstractions, no deploy platform, no heavy runtime dependency
